name: Specify Design Token Management API
what_it_does: Design token management platform with REST API — enabling design teams to extract design tokens from Figma, manage token values centrally, and distribute them to multiple platforms (Web CSS/SCSS, React, iOS, Android, Flutter) via automated pipelines, with version control for tokens and CI/CD integration for token publishing.
use_cases: Extracting design tokens from Figma automatically via Specify API and transforming to platform-specific formats
Managing and versioning design token values centrally via Specify API for multi-platform consistency
Integrating Specify token pipeline into CI/CD for automated token publishing on design changes
Querying token values and history via Specify API for design system audit and governance
Distributing design tokens across repositories and platforms via Specify pipeline automation
not_for: Design documentation and brand guidelines (use Zeroheight or Frontify for documentation sites)
Component development and testing (use Storybook for component development workflow)
Design file creation (use Figma for design work — Specify consumes Figma token output)
best_when: A design system team needs a dedicated design token pipeline that extracts from Figma and auto-distributes to multiple code platforms — without manually writing Style Dictionary configs or custom export scripts.
avoid_when: You have a simple single-platform token need, already have a working Style Dictionary setup, or don't use Figma as design source of truth.

auth.notes: Personal Access Token (PAT) from Specify account settings. Bearer token auth. Organization-scoped access. Webhook for token update and pipeline run events. Figma integration via Figma plugin and OAuth for design sync. JavaScript/TypeScript SDK for custom parsers and templates. CLI for local token pipeline automation. No OAuth2 for Specify API itself.

pricing.paid_tiers: Team $25/month (up to 5 members); Business $75/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: French company (Paris). Founded 2020. Raised $3M seed funding. Backed by Sequoia, Kima Ventures. Design token pipeline specialist. Figma Tokens (now Tokens Studio) competitor. Integration with Style Dictionary for output format flexibility. Open source parser/template system for custom platform outputs. Design token W3C spec alignment. EU company with GDPR-first design. Growing design token management market as design systems mature.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per organization; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for token management and pipeline configuration. PAT bearer auth. Good documentation with JavaScript/TypeScript SDK examples. Figma OAuth required for design token extraction. Webhook for token update events. Custom parsers/templates in JavaScript for advanced platform outputs. Open source components aid in debugging. French company with GDPR focus.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Token reads fully idempotent. Pipeline reads idempotent. Token sync operations are triggered — use sync job ID for status. Token values at specific versions are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Figma OAuth required for token extraction — separate from Specify API token
Custom parser/template system requires JavaScript coding for non-standard outputs
Free tier limited to 1 source — insufficient for multi-source design systems
Token sync is asynchronous — Figma changes take time to propagate through pipeline
Small French startup (2020) — evaluate long-term viability vs. more established alternatives

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. EU data residency. PAT bearer auth. French company with GDPR-first design. Design tokens may contain unreleased brand identities — restrict access. Custom parsers run in pipeline — review parser code in CI/CD. Figma OAuth scopes should be read-only for token extraction.
